title: Introduction to biological macromolecules
description: Test your knowledge of biological macromolecules!

assessment_items.question_content:
[[☃ image 1]]

*Image credit: OpenStax College [CC BY 3.0], via Wikimedia Commons*

**Which of the following best describes the role that water plays in the reaction depicted above?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

options.alt: A diagram of a chemical reaction shows 2 ovals with a plus sign between them. The first oval is orange and labeled Monomer 1 and it has a line with an OH highlighted in blue on the right side of the oval. The second oval is green and labeled Monomer 2 and it has a line with an HO on the left of the oval. The H is highlighted. The arrow points from Monomer 1 plus Monomer 2 towards 2 unlabeled ovals connected to each other by a line from each oval attached to an O between the ovals. Above the arrow is an arrow branching off of the arrow and pointing upward towards water.

choices.content: When water condenses out of the reaction, a dimer is created as a covalent bond forms between two monomers.
Water is an input of the reaction, leading to the formation of a covalent bond in the dimer product.
When water combines with the two monomers, a dimer forms.
Water catalyzes the formation of a covalent bond in the dimer through hydrolysis.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which of the following questions would a researcher most likely ask if they wanted to know whether a hydrolysis reaction or a dehydration synthesis reaction is occurring?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Is water a reactant or product of the reaction?
Are the monomers identical to one another?
Is an enzyme involved in the reaction?
Which polymer is involved in the reaction?

assessment_items.question_content:
[[☃ image 1]]

*Image credit: OpenStax College [CC BY 3.0], via Wikimedia Commons*

**What role do the highlighted hydroxyl group ($\text{--OH}$) and hydrogen atom ($\text{H}$) play in the depicted reaction above?**


[[☃ radio 1]]

options.alt: A diagram of a chemical reaction shows 2 ovals with a plus sign between them. The first oval is orange and labeled Monomer 1 and it has a line with an OH highlighted in blue on the right side of the oval. The second oval is green and labeled Monomer 2 and it has a line with an HO on the left of the oval. The H is highlighted. The arrow points from Monomer 1 plus Monomer 2 towards 2 unlabeled ovals connected to each other by a line from each oval attached to an O between the ovals. Above the arrow is an arrow branching off of the arrow and pointing upward towards water.

choices.content: Through dehydration synthesis, they condense out of the reaction as a water molecule and a covalent bond forms in their place.
Through hydrolysis, they condense out of the reaction as a water molecule and a covalent bond forms in their place.
Through dehydration synthesis, a water molecule is added to those regions of the monomers to produce a covalent bond in the dimer.
Through hydrolysis, a water molecule is added to those regions of the monomers to produce a covalent bond in the dimer.

assessment_items.question_content:
[[☃ image 1]]

In the reaction above, two glucose monomers combine to form the dimer maltose. 

**Which of the following best explains the process of this reaction?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

options.alt: The diagram of a chemical reaction shows two hexagonal shaped molecules both labeled glucose. There is a dotted circle around an OH of one glucose molecule and a H of the other glucose molecule. An arrow points from the glucose to two hexagonal shaped molecules connected to each other by an O with the label maltose. Next to the structure of maltose is a plus sign and water.

choices.content: A covalent bond forms through dehydration synthesis, which results in the formation of maltose and water.
An ionic bond forms through dehydration synthesis, which combines the two glucose monomers and forms the maltose dimer.
To form a covalent bond, the monomers underwent a hydrolysis reaction.
Water is added to the reaction to promote the formation of a covalent bond between the glucose monomers.

assessment_items.question_content:
[[☃ image 1]]

*Image credit: Adapted from OpenStax College [CC BY 3.0], via Wikimedia Commons*

In the reaction depicted above, two amino acids have combined to form a dipeptide. 

**Which of the following best explains the process occuring in this reaction?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

options.alt: The diagram shows the chemical structure of two identical amino acids. The carboxyl group of the first amino acid is next to the amine group of the second amino acid and the OH of the carboxyl group and one hydrogen of the amine group are highlighted by a box. There is an arrow pointing from the two amino acids towards the dipeptide that is formed and the line connecting the Carboxyl carbon of the first amino acid with the Nitrogen of the second amino acid is labeled covalent bond. Next to the chemical formula of the dipeptide is a box labeled plus water.

choices.content: Through dehydration synthesis, a covalent bond forms to connect the dipeptide together, and a water molecule forms as a product.
Water is used to cleave the covalent bond between the two amino acid monomers.
The dipeptide will immediately break back down into the two amino acid monomers.
Through hydrolysis, a covalent bond forms to connect the dipeptide together, and a water molecule forms as a product.

assessment_items.question_content:
[[☃ image 1]]

*Image credit: Adapted from OpenStax College [CC BY 3.0], via Wikimedia Commons*

In the reaction depicted above, two amino acids have combined to form a dipeptide. 

**Which of the following best describes the role of water in the reaction?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

options.alt: The diagram shows the chemical structure of two identical amino acids. The carboxyl group of the first amino acid is next to the amine group of the second amino acid and the OH of the carboxyl group and one hydrogen of the amine group are highlighted by a box. There is an arrow pointing from the two amino acids towards the dipeptide that is formed, and the line connecting the Carboxyl carbon of the first amino acid with the Nitrogen of the second amino acid is labeled covalent bond. Next to the chemical formula of the dipeptide is a box labeled plus water.

choices.content: Water is a product that forms as a result of a dehydration synthesis reaction between the two amino acid monomers.
Water is a reactant that is added in order to drive the dehydration synthesis reaction between the amino acid monomers.
Water is a product that forms as a result of the hydrolysis of the dipeptide.
Waters forms as result of a combination between the amino group ($\text{--NH}_{2}$) of one amino acid and the carboxyl group ($\text{-COOH}$) of the other amino acid.

assessment_items.question_content:
[[☃ image 1]]

*Image credit: OpenStax College [CC BY 3.0], via Wikimedia Commons*

**Which of the following best describes the role that water plays in the reaction depicted above?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

options.alt: A diagram of a chemical reaction shows 2 unlabeled ovals, one orange and one green, connected to each other by an O atom. An arrow points from this to 2 labeled ovals separated by a plus sign. The first oval after the arrow is orange and labeled Monomer 1 and it has a line on the right with an OH attached and highlighted in blue. The second oval is green and labeled Monomer 2 and it has a line to the left with an HO attached. The H is highlighted in blue Above the arrow is a shape labeled water and a curved arrow pointing from the water into the arrow.

choices.content: As a reactant, water cleaves the covalent bond that holds the dimer together.
When water is added, the two monomers form a covalent bond that holds the dimer together.
Water is a product of this dehydration synthesis reaction.
In the reverse of this reaction, water is used to promote hydrolysis.

assessment_items.question_content:
[[☃ image 1]]

*Image credit: OpenStax College [CC BY 3.0], via Wikimedia Commons*

**Based on the reaction depicted above, which of the following statements is true?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

options.alt: A diagram of a chemical reaction shows 2 unlabeled ovals, one orange and one green, connected to each other by an O atom. An arrow points from this to 2 labeled ovals separated by a plus sign. The first oval after the arrow is orange and labeled Monomer 1 and it has a line on the right with an OH attached and highlighted in blue. The second oval is green and labeled Monomer 2 and it has a line to the left with an HO attached. The H is highlighted in blue  Above the arrow is a shape labeled water and a curved arrow pointing from the water into the arrow.

choices.content: If water is not added to the reaction, hydrolysis will not occur.
If water is not added to the reaction, dehydration synthesis will not occur.
The reverse of this reaction requires the addition of water in order to build the dimer.
The reverse of this reaction requires the removal of water from the reaction in order to break down the dimer.

assessment_items.question_content:
[[☃ image 1]]

*Image credit: OpenStax College [CC BY 3.0], via Wikimedia Commons*

**If water was removed from the reaction depicted above, which of the following describes the most likely outcome of this removal?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

options.alt: A diagram of a chemical reaction shows 2 unlabeled ovals, one orange and one green, connected to each other by an O atom. An arrow points from this to 2 labeled ovals separated by a plus sign. The first oval after the arrow is orange and labeled Monomer 1 and it has a line on the right with an OH attached and highlighted in blue. The second oval is green and labeled Monomer 2 and it has a line to the left with an HO attached. The H is highlighted in blue  Above the arrow is a shape labeled water and a curved arrow pointing from the water into the arrow.

choices.content: The covalent bond that holds the dimer together would not be cleaved because hydrolysis would not occur.
The covalent bond that holds the dimer together would not be cleaved because dehydration synthesis would not occur.
The reverse of the reaction would occur and the dimer would spontaneously form from the two monomers.
The reverse of the reaction would occur and a covalent bond would spontaneously form between the two monomers.

assessment_items.question_content:
[[☃ image 1]]

In the reaction depicted above, maltose is broken down into two molecules of glucose. 

**Which of the following best explains the process of this reaction?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

options.alt: The diagram of a chemical reaction shows two hexagonal shaped molecules connected to each other by an O. The structure is labeled maltose. Next to the structure is a plus sign and water highlighted in blue, and then an arrow pointing to two hexagonal shaped molecules that are both labeled glucose. The first glucose molecule has an OH highlighted in blue and the second glucose molecule has an H highlighted in blue.

choices.content: The addition of a water molecule cleaves the covalent bond holding maltose together.
This breakdown process requires the release of water as a product of the reaction.
The ionic bond of maltose is broken down through the addition of water in this hydrolysis reaction.
The reverse of this reaction would require the addition of water to build the maltose dimer from the glucose monomers.

assessment_items.question_content:
Lactose is a dimer sugar composed of two monomeric subunits (galactose and glucose). In an investigation, researchers placed intestinal cells in a nutrient medium containing lactose. The researchers determined that the lactose had broken down to galactose and glucose in the manner depicted in the reaction below.


[[☃ image 1]]

*Image credit: Ed (Edgar181) [Public domain], from Wikimedia Commons*

The cells were then modified and re-exposed to the lactose medium. The researchers were no longer able to detect the presence of galactose and glucose. 

**Which of the following conclusions best explains an outcome of this modification?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

options.alt: The chemical structure of lactose is depicted as 2 hexagonal shaped molecules bonded by an oxygen molecule. An arrow points from lactose to the chemical structure of D-galactose plus D-glucose. Lactase is written above the arrow, and water is written below the arrow.

choices.content: The cells were unable to hydrolyze the lactose dimer into its monomeric subunits.
The cells were able to perform dehydration synthesis reactions in order to break down lactose into its monomeric subunits.
The cells were unable to hydrolyze lactose, resulting in the formation of a covalent bond between galactose and glucose.
The cells were able to undergo a reverse reaction and build lactose.

assessment_items.question_content:
The human body is capable of digesting the complex polymer starch into simpler subunits. This digestive process requires the presence of water and the enzyme amylase in order to break down starch into maltose. This is depicted below.


[[☃ image 1]]

*Image credit: Adapted from BQmUB2012134 [CC], Wikimedia Commons*

**Which of the following statements best describes the digestion of starch into maltose?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

options.alt: The chemical structure of labeled starch is depicted as 4 hexagonal shaped molecules each bonded to the next molecule at an oxygen. An arrow points from starch to two molecules of maltose, both depicted as 2 hexagonal shaped molecules connected to each other by an oxygen. To the left of the arrow amylase is written, and to the right of the arrow there is a line that joins the the arrow labeled water.

choices.content: This is a hydrolysis reaction that requires water to break down a complex polymer into simpler subunits.
This is a hydrolysis reaction that forms water by breaking down a complex polymer into simpler subunits.
This is a dehydration synthesis reaction that requires water to form a complex polymer from simpler subunits.
This is a dehydration synthesis reaction that produces water when forming a complex polymer from simpler subunits.
